wrong: `ava'k noatiyand ikkathe!` (FINAL.md line 2038 — "Don't keep staring at her!")
why: "Staring at her" requires accusative case (direct object), not dative.
text: `ava'k noatiyand ikkathe!` (FINAL.md line 2038 — "Don't keep staring at her!") `avala noatiyand ikkathe!` "Staring at her" requires accusative case (direct object), not dative.

why: The textbook never mentions the word for father at all.
text: bappa (incorrectly assumed to mean "father") appa The textbook never mentions the word for father at all. अप्प

why: gothth = knowledge. Missing someone = ghê-na aapa
text: gothth aapa (I miss them) ghê-na aapa gothth = knowledge. Missing someone = ghê-na aapa

correct: jana (people); mansa = individual man — do NOT use mansa for crowd
text: mansa (used as people/crowd) jana (people); mansa = individual man — do NOT use mansa for crowd

correct: tholipo = to laugh; aado = to dance. These are different.
why: aadiyan = dancing, NOT laughing. Laughing = thoLchandh / tholipo
text: aadiyan (laughing) tholipo = to laugh; aado = to dance. These are different. aadiyan = dancing, NOT laughing. Laughing = thoLchandh / tholipo

text: rasa'da gaaLi uNd (there is an aroma of taste) rasa'thra ghamana injathe Correct genitive -'thra and correct verb form

why: ghama = aroma/smell (not in FINAL.md — native speaker supplied)

wrong: ava baarii chaayi mansa (she is a very good person)
why: chaayi = good (adjective); naLLa jana = good person
text: ava baarii chaayi mansa (she is a very good person) naLLa jana (good person) chaayi = good (adjective); naLLa jana = good person

why: Genitive suffix is -'ra for these nouns, not -'da
text: kuuL'da, karii'da (genitive with food items) kuuL'ra, karii'ra Genitive suffix is -'ra for these nouns, not -'da
kadambuttu'da rasa'na kadambuttu'ra rasa'tna

wrong: bedh'ra neake bisi (hot like the sun)
why: bedh = sunlight; if fire/flame heat intended, use chuduva'cchak
text: bedh'ra neake bisi (hot like the sun) chuduva'cchak (burning like — from fire/flame context) bedh = sunlight; if fire/flame heat intended, use chuduva'cchak

correct: naada = MY (1st person possessive only). āāwuNda = his

note: 'nda suffix = with/accompanied by. Different from 'k (dative = to/toward)
text: appa'k used for "with father" appa'nda (companion/instrumental case) 'nda suffix = with/accompanied by. Different from 'k (dative = to/toward)
